description_long: This bird features a striking red crown atop its head, contrasting sharply with its predominantly black upperparts and wings. The underparts are lighter, with white patches visible along the flanks and under the wings, and subtle mottling on the breast. The beak is long, straight, and chisel-shaped, ideal for pecking wood. The tail is relatively long and appears wedge-shaped, with dark feathers and a slightly notched tip. The legs are short and dark, mostly obscured by the body. The overall appearance is robust and streamlined, with a distinctive color pattern that makes it easily recognizable in urban parks or wooded neighborhoods.
description: A medium-sized bird with a vivid red crown, black upperparts, white underparts, and a long, chisel-shaped beak. Its tail is wedge-shaped and the plumage is boldly patterned.
description_view_0: Side view showing the red crown, black back, white underparts, and long beak.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the dark, wedge-shaped tail and the red crown at the top of the head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with similar features: red crown, black back, and white underparts, with the beak visible.
description_view_3: Front view emphasizing the pointed beak, red crown, and the symmetry of the facial markings.
attribute_plumage_pattern: Solid black upperparts with a bright red crown, white patches on the face and underparts, and some mottling on the breast.
